name: Puerto del León Loop from Málaga
name_original: Montes de Málaga – circular desde Málaga
description: This difficult 32.4-mile touring cycling loop from Málaga offers challenging climbs, pine forests, and panoramic views of the city and coast
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around La Axarquía feature a varied topography, including a rugged coastline, dramatic mountains, and fertile valleys. The region is characterized by the Parque Natural de las Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama, offering challenging ascents and panoramic views. Cyclists can explore picturesque white villages and routes around Lake Viñuela, with terrain ranging from coastal paths to mountain roads. Best touring cycling routes around La Axarquía The most popular touring cycling route is Puerto del León Loop from Málaga, a 32.3 miles (52.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 54 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Nerja, Frigiliana, and Torrox Loop, a difficult 20.4 miles (32.9 km) path. This route connects coastal towns with traditional white villages, offering sea views and cultural exploration. Local touring cyclists also love the Axarquía Raisin Route Cycle Loop, a 25.3 miles (40.8 km) trail leading through scenic mountains and traditional villages, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Touring cycling around La Axarquía is defined by coastal paths, mountainous terrain, and routes through white villages. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate coastal rides to challenging mountain climbs. The routes in La Axarquía are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 2,200 reviews. More than 25,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore La Axarquía's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in La Axarquía?
A: La Axarquía offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 500 options available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in La Axarquía?
A: The region's topography is incredibly varied. You'll find routes along the rugged coastline, through fertile valleys with orchards, and into dramatic mountains like the Parque Natural de las Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama. This diversity means you can experience everything from well-paved roads to unpaved sections suitable for gravel or mountain biking.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in La Axarquía?
A: La Axarquía boasts over 300 days of sunshine annually and mild winters, making it an ideal destination for year-round cycling. Even in summer, early morning rides offer cooler temperatures. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant for exploring the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, La Axarquía has over 100 easy touring cycling routes. For a relaxed ride, consider options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40352217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Caleta Seafront Promenade – La Malagueta Beach loop from La Unión&lt;/a&gt;, which offers gentle terrain and coastal views.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists in La Axarquía?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, La Axarquía offers nearly 300 difficult routes. The mountainous terrain, particularly in the Parque Natural de las Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama, provides significant elevation gains. A demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962193965/puerto-del-leon-loop-from-malaga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del León Loop from Málaga&lt;/a&gt;, featuring over 1,100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through the famous white villages of La Axarquía?
A: Yes, many routes are designed to showcase La Axarquía's picturesque white villages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981456115/nerja-frigiliana-and-torrox-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nerja, Frigiliana, and Torrox Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, connecting coastal towns with traditional white villages like Frigiliana, known for its Moorish charm.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in La Axarquía?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976048996/via-verde-del-rincon-de-la-victoria-hasta-torre-del-mar-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Rincón de la Victoria hasta Torre del Mar – circular&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate ride, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981469026/embalse-la-vinuela-circular-desde-periana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse La Viñuela – circular desde Periana&lt;/a&gt; provides a more challenging loop around the scenic Lake Viñuela.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I explore along touring cycling routes in La Axarquía?
A: Beyond the stunning natural landscapes, you can discover historical sites and natural monuments. Routes often pass by highlights such as the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7262649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba of Málaga&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/393620" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Viñuela Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or the protected &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6708050" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalhorce Estuary Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;. The region's white villages themselves are significant cultural landmarks.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in La Axarquía?
A: While many routes involve climbs, there are options suitable for families, especially along the coast or on flatter sections. Look for routes marked as 'easy' difficulty, which typically have less elevation gain and are shorter in distance. The coastal paths offer a safer and more enjoyable experience for families.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about cycling in La Axarquía?
A: The touring cycling routes in La Axarquía are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 2,200 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse scenery, from coastal views to mountain panoramas, and the charm of the traditional white villages. The region's favorable climate also makes it a popular choice for year-round riding.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that explore the area around Lake Viñuela?
A: Yes, Lake Viñuela is a popular focal point for cycling. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981469026/embalse-la-vinuela-circular-desde-periana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse La Viñuela – circular desde Periana&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that loops around the lake, offering picturesque views and serving as a gateway to uphill climbs into the natural park.

Q: Can I find routes that follow specific themes, like the Raisin Route?
A: Yes, La Axarquía is known for its themed routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981379239/axarquia-raisin-route-cycle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Axarquía Raisin Route Cycle Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, leading through scenic mountains and traditional villages associated with the region's raisin production, offering both cultural insight and beautiful landscapes.
tips:
The lighthouse in Malaga, known as La Farola de Malaga, is an iconic landmark of the Andalusian port city. It is located at the port of Malaga and is one of the few lighthouses in Spain named in the feminine form. “Farola” means “The Lighthouse Woman”. La Farola was built in 1817 and has served as a landmark for ships heading to the port ever since. Over the years, the lighthouse has been modernized to meet the demands of modern shipping. It stands on the Paseo de la Farola, a popular promenade that runs along the port. In addition to its practical function, the Farola also has symbolic value and represents the maritime spirit of Malaga. 

The lighthouse itself is about 38 meters high and has a very characteristic cylindrical shape, making it an unmistakable element of the Malaga skyline. Especially at sunset, the area around the lighthouse offers beautiful views of the sea and the city. A great destination for a tour in Malaga.

The Muelle Uno is a lively harbor promenade in Málaga that is well worth seeing. The modern area stretches along the harbor and connects the old town of Málaga with the Mediterranean. Muelle Uno offers a mix of shopping, gastronomy, culture and leisure activities in a unique maritime atmosphere. The promenade is particularly lively in the evenings, when the lights of the harbor bathe the surroundings in a special atmosphere.

At the end of the Muelle Uno is the La Farola de Málaga lighthouse, which further decorates the area. Another attraction is the Centre Pompidou Málaga, which is characterized by its striking, colorful glass cube design and is a branch of the famous Parisian museum. It displays contemporary art and attracts culture enthusiasts from all over the world.

El Muelle Uno is a great place to enjoy harbor life and relax - with wonderful views of the Mediterranean and the city. A great tour destination that should not be missed when cycling in the region.
Nice climb, as a cyclist you are not alone. Always nice to meet other cyclists.

The winding approach, which runs through the Montes Natural Park with its picturesque surroundings, makes the tour an ideal route for cyclists and mountain bikers looking for a sporting challenge and impressive views. The route offers many climbs and sections with descents that are both demanding and varied. The tour is particularly suitable for experienced riders, it offers a perfect combination of sporting challenge, scenic beauty and truly magnificent views.

Fuente de la Reina is a famous viewpoint and popular destination for local cyclists in Málaga, where a spring is located. It is located in the Montes de Málaga, at about 900 meters above sea level, and is about 16 kilometers away from the city center. The route to Fuente de la Reina has been compared to the infamous Alpe d'Huez in France for cyclists

Puerto del León is a climb in the Montes de Málaga, a mountain range in the Costa del Sol region. It is a popular route for cyclists due to its beautiful landscapes and relatively constant gradient.
The climb is approximately 16 kilometres long.
The climb covers 894 metres of altitude.
The average gradient is 5.6%, with a steepest section of 11.8%.
From the top you can enjoy beautiful views of the city of Málaga and its surroundings.
Good place to enjoy magnificent views of the Malaga coast
Passport with passport plate. What more do you want. Good view. You can find it here too.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Puerto del León Loop from Málaga for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This loop is considered a difficult touring bicycle route, suitable for cyclists with at least a good fitness level. The main climb to Puerto del León is about 15-16 km long with an elevation gain of approximately 875-900 meters. While the average gradient is around 5.6%, you'll encounter varied gradients, including steep sections up to 10-14%, and even a 17% ramp if you choose to climb via Olías first. An e-bike can make the route accessible to most riders.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of paved roads, making it suitable for touring bikes. While generally well-maintained, some sections, especially in the city at the start, might have mud, stones, or rubbish after rain. The descent from Puerto del León towards Olías is described as technical, with numerous unpredictable corners and a rather narrow road, requiring caution.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several stunning viewpoints and landmarks. Early in the ride, you'll pass La Farola of Málaga and the Muelle Uno Promenade. As you ascend, look for the Montes Road and the View of Málaga from El Mirador, which offers magnificent vistas of the city and the Bay of Málaga. Further along, you'll reach León Pass and another View of Málaga Bay, providing broad panoramas of the coastline and surrounding mountains.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Puerto del León Loop?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures. Summer can be very hot, adding to the challenge, while winter might bring cooler temperatures and potentially more unpredictable weather. Be mindful of heat and wind, which can significantly impact your ride at different times of the year.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities or places to stop for refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are roadside 'ventas' (restaurants) and a popular rest stop called Venta Galwey along the route. You'll also find two historical fountains where you can refill your water bottles, which is especially useful on warmer days.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead for this loop?
faqs.answer: The loop typically starts from Málaga city. While specific parking details aren't provided, you would generally look for parking within Málaga city near the start of the route, such as around the port area or city center, and cycle out. Public transport options within Málaga can also get you close to the starting point.

faqs.question: Is the Puerto del León Loop from Málaga dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a touring bicycle route on paved roads, it's generally not suitable for dogs to accompany cyclists directly on the trail due to traffic and the nature of the activity. If you're looking for dog-friendly outdoor activities in the Montes de Málaga Natural Park, consider hiking trails where dogs are permitted, often on a leash.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle through the Parque Natural Montes de Málaga?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or specific permits required for cycling on the public roads that traverse the Parque Natural Montes de Málaga. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice leave-no-trace principles to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling or hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially overlaps with several other paths. Notable sections include parts of [CIMA ML10] El León * Totalán (42%), [CIMA ML10] El León * Málaga (31%), and [CIMA ML10] El León * Olías (25%). You'll also briefly encounter sections of La Senda Litoral (16%) and Málaga - Rincón de la Victoria (16%) near the city.

faqs.question: What are the natural features and scenery like on the Puerto del León Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop offers diverse and stunning scenery. You'll pass through the beautiful Montes de Málaga Natural Park, characterized by extensive Aleppo pine forests that provide welcome shade. The route features panoramic views of the city of Málaga, the Axarquía region, the Mijas mountain range, and the coastline. You'll also encounter unique elements like two 360-degree loops and tiny tunnels, and pass through the charming white village of Olías on the descent.

faqs.question: Is there a strategic way to ride this loop to make it easier or more challenging?
faqs.answer: Yes, the loop can be ridden in both directions, offering distinct experiences. If you ride the loop in reverse, climbing via Olías first, you might encounter a steep 1km ramp known as 'El Muro de Olías' with sections up to 17%, adding a significant challenge. Riding it as described (climbing to Puerto del León first) is the classic approach, but reversing it offers a different perspective and a tougher climb.

waypoints.name: View of Málaga from El Mirador
